zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Love於1489年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可貝可萊斯時，她的父親Richard Love16歲，母親Agnes Crane22歲。她於1509年與William Barrett在英國艾可艾維勒結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1575年在英國素福爾可維斯塔爾享年86歲。

nameMeanings.name: Love
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from a Middle English personal name derived from the Old English female name Lufu ‘love’, or the masculine equivalent Lufa. Compare Leaf 2.

English and Scottish: nickname from Anglo-Norman French love ‘she-wolf’ or simply ‘wolf’, the word love being a back-formation from the diminutive forms lovel and lovet ‘little wolf’. See also Low 3, from Anglo-Norman French lou, the more usual form of the word.
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhionghuin (see McKinnon ).

variants.name: Lovell
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lovel ‘wolf cub’, a diminutive of lou, love ‘wolf’. Compare Love and Low .

variants.name: Lovett
meanings: from Anglo-Norman French louet ‘wolf-cub’, a diminutive of lou ‘wolf’. The nickname may also have been used as a personal name. Compare Love , Lovell , Low .
from the Middle English female personal name Luvot or Lovet, pet forms of Love .

variants.name: Lauve
meanings: Altered form, under the French influence in LA, of English   Love  .

variants.name: Leaf
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English lef, leff, lief, life ‘beloved’ (Old English lēof ‘beloved; dear one, sweetheart’).

English: occasionally perhaps from a Middle English personal name Lef. This could represent either Old Norse Leifr (see Leif 1) or Old English Lēof which, though unrecorded, is a very probable short form of Old English names such as Lēofrīc, Lēofwine. Compare Leif 3.
Americanized form of Swedish Lööf, Löf, or Löv: ornamental name from löv ‘leaf’. Compare Leif 2.

variants.name: Lovegrove
meanings: English (Berkshire): habitational name from an unidentified place, possibly named with the Old English personal name Lufa (see Love 1) + Old English grāf ‘grove, thicket’.

variants.name: Luff
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Love   1–3.
German: from Luffo, a pet form a personal name such as Ludolph .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Love was born in 1489, in Beccles, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard Love, was 16 and her mother, Agnes Crane, was 22. She married William Barrett in 1509, in Aveley, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 7 daughters. She died in 1575, in Westhall, Suffolk, England, at the age of 86.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Love est née en 1489, à Beccles, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard Love, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Agnes Crane, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé William Barrett en 1509, à Aveley, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée en 1575, à Westhall, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 86 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Love 1489 in Beccles, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard Love 16 und ihre Mutter Agnes Crane 22. Sie hat 1509 in Aveley, Essex, England William Barrett geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb 1575 im Alter von 86 Jahren in Westhall, Suffolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Love è nata il nel 1489 nel Beccles, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Richard Love, aveva 16 anni e sua madre, Agnes Crane, aveva 22 anni. Lei sposato William Barrett nel 1509, nel Aveley, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 7 figlie. Morì nel 1575, nel Westhall, Suffolk, England, all'età di 86 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Loveが1489年にイギリスイギリススフォルクベククレスで生まれた時，父Richard Loveは16歳，母Agnes Craneは22歳であった。1509年にイギリスエセックスエイベリーでWilliam Barrettと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1575年にイギリススフォルクウエスサルで86歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Love(이)가 1489년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 베c클스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Love(은)는 16세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Agnes Crane(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1509년 영국 에세스 에벨리에서 William Barrett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1575년 영국 수폴크 웨스솔에서 86세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Love nasceu em 1489, em Beccles, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard Love, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Crane, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com William Barrett em 1509, em Aveley, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1575, em Westhall, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 86 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Love родилась в 1489 г. в местности бекклесь, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard Love было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Crane, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Barrett в 1509 г. в местности авели, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 7 дочерей. Она умерла в 1575 г. в местности вестолл, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 86 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Love nació en 1489, en Beccles, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Richard Love, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Agnes Crane, tenía 22 años. Se casó con William Barrett en 1509, en Aveley, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió en 1575, en Westhall, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 86 años.
